item: verba/base64 — `base64url_encode` exported at line 96 with no decode partner; header lies "URL-safe variant deferred" while the function already exists; `base64url_decode` is completely absent.
reachability_fact: `base64url_encode` is live-exported at STDLIB/iii/verba/base64.iii:96 (`fn base64url_encode ... @export`). It emits UNPADDED output: rem==1 exits after 2 chars with no `=` (line 116), rem==2 after 3 chars with no `=` (lines 122-125). The existing `base64_decode` at line 169 requires `(src_len - i) == 0` — it unconditionally rejects any non-multiple-of-4 input — so any unpadded URL-safe output is undecidable by the only in-tree decode function. No caller exists for `base64url_encode` anywhere in the corpus (grep `base64url` across corpus confirms zero hits). The header at lines 5-6 states "Standard alphabet only ... URL-safe variant deferred" — a false claim, because the encoder is already present. The Public API block at lines 8-10 documents only `base64_encode`/`base64_decode` — it omits `base64url_encode` entirely, as if the function does not exist. This is the exact doc-says-deferred / impl-exists / decode-absent placeholder shape the backlog item targets.
maximal_design:
Implement `base64url_decode` as a full RFC 4648 §5 URL-safe decoder, unpadded variant, matching the exact output contract of `base64url_encode`:

ALPHABET MAPPING — `b64url_decode_6bit(c: u32) -> u32`:
  A-Z (65-90) → 0..25; a-z (97-122) → 26..51; 0-9 (48-57) → 52..61;
  `-` (45) → 62; `_` (95) → 63; `=` (61), `+` (43), `/` (47) → 0xFF.
  Critically, `+` and `/` (standard alphabet) are BADCHAR in url mode, and `=` is BADCHAR because the encoder never emits padding.

TAIL HANDLING (the structural difference from `base64_decode`):
  After the full 4-char main loop, check `remainder = (src_len - i) % 4`:
    - 0: clean end, no tail.
    - 2: 2 chars → 1 byte. d0=decode(c0), d1=decode(c1); push (d0<<2)|(d1>>4).
    - 3: 3 chars → 2 bytes. d0,d1,d2; push (d0<<2)|(d1>>4) and ((d1<<4)|(d2>>2))&0xFF.
    - 1: impossible — a single 6-bit sextet cannot encode a byte boundary — return B64_E_BADPAD.

BADCHAR propagation: any 0xFF return from `b64url_decode_6bit` during any group (4-char or tail) returns B64_E_BADCHAR immediately.

CONSTANTS: share B64_OK, B64_E_BADCHAR, B64_E_BADPAD, B64_E_BUILD — already defined.

HEADER FIX: lines 5-6 must change from "Standard alphabet only ... URL-safe variant deferred" to accurately describe both standard and url-safe encode+decode. Public API block must be extended to list `base64url_encode` and `base64url_decode`.

This makes the codec strictly better: the URL-safe half is symmetric (encode+decode), the header is truthful, the module exports four complete codec functions, and nothing existing changes signature.
kat:
Corpus file: STDLIB/corpus/1000_base64url_kat.iii

POSITIVE assertions (round-trips that would silently fail without the new function):
- T1: encode([0xFB,0xFF,0xFF]) == "-___" (4 chars); asserts enc3[0]==45('-') and enc3[1]==95('_'), proving sextets 62/63 are actually emitted — a round-trip of "foobar" would never reach these sextets and would be a vacuous test.
- T2: encode([0xF8]) == "-A" (2 chars, tail-2 path); decode("-A") == [0xF8].
- T3: encode([0xFB,0xFF]) == "-_8" (3 chars, tail-3 path); decode("-_8") == [0xFB,0xFF].

NEGATIVE assertions (prove the fix FIRES on bad input):
- N1: base64_decode (STANDARD) on "-___" returns non-zero (BADCHAR). Proves '-' is rejected by the standard decoder — the two alphabets are genuinely distinct.
- N2: base64url_decode on "+Aaa" returns non-zero. Proves '+' (standard alphabet char, sextet 62 in standard) is BADCHAR in url mode — the alphabet tables are different functions, not shared.
- N3: base64url_decode on "AA==" returns non-zero. Proves '=' is BADCHAR in unpadded url mode — a decoder that silently skips padding would pass N1/N2 but fail here.
- N4: base64url_decode on "AAAA" + 1 extra char (5-byte input) returns non-zero (BADPAD). Proves a 1-char tail is structurally rejected — a decoder that silently drops it would pass all prior tests.
